c4b6.summary: Use group-clustered JSON files instead of a flat zmem-style graph
c4b6.content:
## Status
accepted

## Context
zmem stored all nodes in a single graph.json which caused:
- Slow YAML processing (confirmed by user)
- Monolithic structure hard to extend
- No per-group backend flexibility

## Decision
Break memory into 4 default groups (project_dna, problem_tracking, decision_trail, code_history), each with its own JSON file and optionally its own backend provider.

## Consequences
+ Groups can independently use json/sqlite/vector backends
+ Clear semantic boundaries reduce cross-group noise
- Queries across groups require a fan-out step via MemStore

7dd1.summary: orjson as hard dependency, MtimeFileCache per JsonBackend
7dd1.content:
## Status
accepted

## Context
zmem used YAML which the user found notably slow in practice. orjson is 3-6x faster than stdlib json for typical payloads.

## Decision
orjson is a required dependency (not optional). All JSON reads go through MtimeFileCache (keyed by st_mtime float) to avoid redundant disk reads during scan/export within a single process lifetime.

## Consequences
+ Much faster repeated reads in scan/export flows
+ Cache invalidation is automatic on mtime change or explicit via invalidate()
- orjson requires a C extension; pure-Python fallback removed

21fd.summary: zhar stack uses zuu.GhCacheDir for bucket management — TTL-based clone/pull cache
21fd.content:
## Status
proposed

## Context
zuu provides GhCacheDir: TTL-based GitHub repo cache handling clone-or-pull. Requires Python >=3.12, conflicts with zhar current >=3.10.

## Decision
Raise zhar minimum to 3.12, add zuu as dependency. GhCacheDir.ensure() handles full lifecycle.

## Open
- Confirm 3.12 min acceptable
- Confirm sync always re-resolves via GhCacheDir TTL

1782.summary: stack template: AND/OR/NOT compound conditions (capitalized, spaced); + shorthand for AND in branch refs

5dc4.summary: stack template: stacked %ZIF% = standard nesting; each %ZIF% opens scope, %ZC% closes innermost

1a8f.summary: Stack implementation validated end-to-end: 442 tests passing across template, bucket, registry, sync, and CLI flows
1a8f.content:
## Result

The stack feature set is implemented end-to-end and currently verifies cleanly at 442/442 tests passing.

## Coverage highlights

- Template engine: 39 focused tests covering parsing, branching semantics, inline text, raw paste, eval, and compound conditions.
- Bucket manager: 16 focused tests covering cache add, resolve, list, and remove behavior.
- Registry: 17 focused tests covering install, uninstall, lookup, listing, and persistence in .zhar/cfg/stack.json.
- Sync: 12 focused tests covering rendering, output naming, dry-run mode, and per-item error isolation.
- CLI wiring is present for stack bucket add/list/remove, install, uninstall, list, and sync --dry-run.

## Interpretation

This establishes the current stack implementation as functionally complete for the planned template, bucket, registry, sync, and CLI surfaces described in code_history and decision_trail.

014f.summary: GroupDef runtime_context_providers let each group gather external tool context (including subprocess-backed providers) during export/install
